macro.title: NAHB data maps modest housing affordability gains in 2025
macro.what: NAHB index shows Q4 2025 new home payment at 34% of income, as new home prices fell 2.0% and rates eased to 6.0%.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Back on the Market: Relistings Jump as Home Sellers Bet on Stronger Spring Market
macro.what: Nearly 45,000 sellers who delisted their homes last year relisted them in January—the highest January number in records dating back a decade
macro.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
macro.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

macro.title: House Hunters Stayed on Sidelines As Rates Dipped Below 6%, Iran War Adds to Market Uncertainty
macro.what: High prices and economic uncertainty kept demand muted; now, rates are already rising again and global tensions could add to homebuyer hesitation
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Redfin Economists’ Weekly Take: After Dipping Below 6%, Mortgage Rates Enter Unpredictable Week Amid Iran Tensions
macro.what: This Week In A Nutshell: We’re in for an unpredictable week with potentially significant moves in rates between the ongoing Iran conflict and important job market data on Friday
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Builders started 2026 with margin pressure, then came Iran war risk

housing.title: Better launches ChatGPT-based credit decision engine for mortgage and HELOC lenders
housing.what: Better.com has launched a conversational credit decision engine that lets mortgage and home equity lenders run underwriting through ChatGPT using the company’s Tinman AI platform.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: NAR speaks out on “coming soon” listings
housing.what: NAR says it has no national “coming soon” policy, leaving listing status rules to local MLSs and emphasizing MLS benefits for consumers and competition.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: New eXp division bridges homebuilder-brokerage service gap
housing.what: eXp New Homes is designed to help agents navigate the complexities of the development process while delivering a higher level of service.
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: Compromise Indiana housing reform law trims local zoning power
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: For Real Estate Investors, the West Coast Is Hot and Florida Is Not
housing.what: Investor activity is sluggish on a national level, with purchases rising just 2%—but it varies widely from metro to metro
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.

housing.title: The Typical U.S. Homeowner Hangs Onto Their House For 12 Years. In Los Angeles, It’s 20 Years.
housing.what: Homeowner tenure peaked at 13.4 years in 2020, roughly doubling the average tenure from 2005
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.

housing.title: West Palm Beach’s Luxury Housing Market Is Booming, With Sales Up 30%
housing.what: Luxury pending home sales in West Palm Beach posted a larger gain than any other major metro in January, rising almost six times faster than non luxury sales
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

notables.title: USDA lending changes could handcuff low-income California homebuyers
notables.what: USDA Section 502 Direct Loan caps drop to 60% of local FHA limits, raising concerns about rural homeownership access in California.

notables.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.
